entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Death of three sisters spotlights India dowry violence
  • 2-year-old accidentally shoots and kills father in Florida
  • Sony's former chief executive Idei dies, aged 84
  • British journalist, indigenous expert missing in Brazil
  • BOJ's Kuroda says weak yen benefits economy if moves aren't too sharp
  • US official says will respond swiftly in case of North Korea nuclear test
  • Racial abuse of England players after Euro final complicates shootouts, says Southgate
  • Businesses need to incorporate decarbonisation, climate risks into strategies to remain competitive: Teo Chee Hean
  • Biden to unveil economic partnership for Americas: US official
  • Australia are used to the hard road to the World Cup: Arnold
  • Japan's Nidec to unify chip procurement amid supply crunch
  • Swedish government faces tough no-confidence vote
  • Bonds slide, yen dumped as interest rate hikes loom
  • Philippines May CPI hits 3-year high, supports further rate hike
  • Southeast Asia 'well short' of 1.5 degrees Celsius climate change goals: Bain, Temasek report
  • Afghan leader unlikely to have fled Kabul with millions in cash: US watchdog
  • Dollar extends 20-year high vs yen amid inflation jitters; Aussie slips before RBA
  • Travelling during the June holidays? Here's how you can mosquito-proof your home
  • Colombia discovers two historical shipwrecks in Caribbean
  • BOJ Kuroda defends remark on households' acceptance of price hikes
  • Don't call us pro-China, Taiwan opposition chief says in US
  • Commentary: Huge benefits but many challenges to high-speed rail between Bangkok and Kuala Lumpur
  • Myanmar junta slams France over 'illegitimate' snub, threatens ties
  • Vietnam minister, Hanoi mayor expelled from party over corruption scandal
  • Bangladesh cancels top human rights group's licence
  • Shanghai residents chafe under fresh COVID-19 lockdowns
  • China warns Australia risks 'serious consequences' after jet interception
  • Indonesia ferry sinking death toll rises to 19 as search called off
  • China warns Canada over air patrols on lookout for North Korea sanctions busting
  • Cambodia's ruling party wins local commune elections but new opposition gains
  • Ousted board of Philippine casino sues Japanese tycoon Okada after resort seized
  • Indonesian parliament expected to finalise the rules for 2024 general election this week
  • India rushes to quell outrage after insulting remarks on Islam
  • Malaysia king urges end to 'political issues', says country must avoid confidence deficit
  • Bangladesh depot accused over blast that killed at least 49
  • Civil service pay rise can have 'ripple effect' on job market, say HR experts
  • Oil prices edge higher on relaxed China COVID curbs, tight supplies
  • Bain Capital closes $2 billion Asia 'special situations fund'
  • Japan Finance Minister: no comment on Kuroda's remark on households accepting of price hikes
  • Philippines' North Star defers IPO on inflation, market concerns
  • Jane Street Global sues LME for $15.3 million for cancelling nickel trades
  • Japan's household spending falls as rising costs squeeze consumers
  • Japan April real wages fall most in four months as inflation jumps
  • Ukraine may give English status of business language: Prime minister
  • Apple unveils new MacBook Air, message recall, other 'wish list' features
  • New York bill aims to limit crypto miners using fossil fuel-generated power
  • Meta names Guy Rosen chief information security officer
  • SCDF rescues dog from Compassvale Bow unit following kitchen fire
  • Two men investigated for causing public alarm after shotgun-like object seen in online video
  • ‘Minimalist’ Cabinet reshuffle cements Lawrence Wong's position as PM-in-waiting: Analysts
  • Sex abuse by prominent Catholic figure: AGC did not apply to lift, vary gag order so as to protect victims
  • 'Give every ounce of my strength to serve Singapore': Lawrence Wong on being appointed Deputy Prime Minister
  • Motorists advised to avoid roads near Shangri-La Hotel during security dialogue from Jun 10 to 12
  • NTUC deputy secretary-general Chee Hong Tat to return to Government full-time, Desmond Tan to join labour movement
  • NTU president Subra Suresh to step down in December after 5 years at the helm
  • Grab delivery cyclist who hit elderly man at void deck gets jail
  • Singapore could expect new Omicron COVID-19 wave in July or August as antibodies wane: Ong Ye Kung
  • Two men caught on video fighting outside Great World arrested for affray
  • Pilot helpline for seniors launched in Ang Mo Kio, with plans to expand it nationwide
  • Lawrence Wong promoted to Deputy Prime Minister in Singapore Cabinet reshuffle
  • Temasek launches investment platform company GenZero in global decarbonisation push
  • Moving company manager fined for illegally disposing of furniture, household waste
  • NDP 2022: CAAS to establish temporary restricted areas over Singapore for aerial activities
  • Two men charged over stabbing victim with 20cm harpoon at Ming Arcade
  • New Zealand apologises to player for 'systematic failings' on wellbeing
  • Canada returns to practice after refusing to play Panama over contract dispute
  • Denmark edge Austria after power outage in Vienna delays game
  • Reshuffled France held by Croatia in Nations League
  • Third competitor dies in 2022 Isle of Man TT races
  • Mickelson to end hiatus and play inaugural LIV Golf event
  • Phelps, Vonn headline Team USA Hall of Fame's Class of 2022
  • New Zealand's De Grandhomme out of England tests with heel injury
  • Belgium's Lukaku out of Poland game due to ankle injury
  • Southgate says Germany the benchmark for England ahead of Munich match
  • Qatar to reinstall Zidane statue that sparked backlash
  • Hwang and Son on target as South Koreans down 10-man Chile
  • I would probably not have played any other Grand Slam with my injury, says Nadal after French win
  • Neymar strikes from penalty spot to see off Japan
  • French Open champion Nadal up to fourth in ATP rankings
  • Swiatek extends lead on top of WTA rankings
  • England name wings May and Nowell in training squad
  • Five more new faces in latest England training camp
  • Gun attack on church in southwest Nigeria leaves 21 dead
  • New York raises age for owning semiautomatic rifle
  • 59% backing is a 'decisive' win: UK PM Boris Johnson
  • In the last-chance saloon, Boris Johnson survives as UK PM for now
  • Commentary: Supply chain middlemen distort global markets and drain revenues
  • Dominican Republic minister shot dead in office
  • Ukraine's Zelenskyy says UK providing Kyiv with 'exactly' the right weapons
  • Mexico snub throws Americas' summit into disarray
  • UK's Boris Johnson scrapes win in party confidence vote; authority is damaged
  • Tunisian president cuts judges' wages after they strike over sackings
  • US wins authority to seize Russian oligarch's planes
CNA: Breaking News, Singapore News, World and Asia

entry.title: Washington Post: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine’s position has ‘worsened’ in fight for Severodonetsk
  • Cholera fears prompt quarantine in Mariupol, official says
  • Fighting in Severodonetsk has ‘worsened,’ official says; Britain pledges rocket systems
  • Boris Johnson survives but is weakened by no-confidence vote
  • Who would replace Boris Johnson? Here are some of the contenders.
  • How Boris Johnson went from landslide victory to no-confidence vote
  • Milley equates ‘horrors’ in Ukraine with suffering during World War II
  • Church attack rattles area of Nigeria where religious violence is rare
  • China secretly building PLA naval facility in Cambodia, Western officials say
  • 49 killed and hundreds injured in Bangladesh container depot fire
  • U.S. and South Korea respond to North Korean launch with 8 missiles of their own
  • Putin threatens new targets if Ukraine gets longer-range rockets; missiles hit Kyiv
  • Millions of Ukrainian refugees may stay in E.U., top official says
  • Migrant caravan sets out in southern Mexico
  • Cabinet minister in Dominican Republic slain in his office
  • EU blames Russia for food crisis, Russian UN envoy walks out
  • Cyprus labor minister dies after suffering brain aneurism
  • Mexico president's party wins 4 of 6 governorships on ballot
  • British journalist, Brazilian expert missing in the Amazon
  • US media face Russian visa denial due to US restrictions
  • Nigerian forces hunt for gunmen who killed 50 at church
  • Russian opera 'Boris Godunov' to open next La Scala season
  • Burglars cut through wall to rob bank deposit boxes in Iran
  • Families sue Uvalde gunman, signal possible action against gunmaker
  • Too liberal for California?
  • More women than ever own guns. Could that change gun laws?
  • There have been over 200 mass shootings so far in 2022
  • A mother’s desperate bid to save her anguished teen ends in gunfire
  • After shooting, Uvalde residents say need for mental health care is high
  • Anger and heartbreak on Bus No. 15
  • Man drowns as Arizona police watch: ‘I’m not jumping in after you’
  • Pete Buttigieg says blaming doorways for school shootings is ‘insanity’
  • Children’s museum apologizes for selling Juneteenth watermelon salad
  • ‘When it’s a shooting on a city street, nothing happens’
  • A liberal DA finds voters’ moods have changed even in San Francisco
  • Mexico’s president snubs Biden’s invitation to summit
  • Proud Boys leader Tarrio, 4 lieutenants charged with seditious conspiracy
  • Post Politics Now: Jan. 6, guns on the radar for Congress; Biden announces latest baby formula flight
  • Elon Musk threatens to back out of Twitter deal over withholding data
  • White House takes steps to spur solar industry
  • ‘We’re all afraid’: Massive rent increases hit mobile homes
  • Taser maker proposed shock drones for schools. What could go wrong?
  • Apple unveils new MacBooks, iOS 16 features and more
  • Dave Smith, whose Prophet-5 synthesizer powered ’80s pop, dies at 72
  • Common lease terms renters should know before signing on dotted line
  • Will a squabble between siblings over a home lead to an eviction?
  • Two-bedroom, two-bath condo in Reston lists at $399,000
  • Brazil Points the Way Out of Inflation. Yes, You Read That Right
  • Western ‘Unity’ Is Making the Ukraine War Worse
  • In Sri Lanka, Even the Rajapaksa Heartland Is Broken
  • An Old-Money Billionaire Rises up Against Adani
  • What’s Behind Vietnam’s Latest Anti-Corruption Fight
  • What the Jan. 6 Committee Has Done, and What’s Next
  • ‘Fake’ Aluminum Stocks Put Perils of China’s Commodities Funding in Spotlight
  • Here’s How US States Differ on Carrying Guns in Public
  • Princeton’s warning to its campus community: Speak at your own risk
  • Metro is short on trains, drivers — and public confidence
  • The question is no longer whether Boris Johnson goes, but when
  • The GOP spin on gun rights is wrong — morally and legally
  • We’ll get less than half a loaf on gun control. We should take it.
  • A new low in anti-trans bills: Genital exams for female athletes
  • This Pride Month, I’m embracing ‘rainbow capitalism’
  • What the Arizona GOP’s response to Uvalde reveals
  • Why losing the midterms isn’t the worst thing for Democrats
  • It’s common sense to raise the minimum age for purchasing rifles
  • Biden must speak truth to Saudi Arabia’s powerful on human rights
  • Most Americans don’t accept the mass slaughter of children. Why does the GOP?
  • Memo to Democrats: Hammer the GOP as complicit in Trump’s coup
  • Why San Francisco’s district attorney recall vote isn’t what it seems
  • The California primary reminds us why our politics are broken
  • No school should have to close because of extreme heat
  • Free speech can’t trump every other value on campus
  • Cartoon by Joe Heller
  • The EPA needs resources — and guidelines
  • D.C.'s sneaky traffic cameras — and appeals process
  • A harrowing story of heroin addiction
  • Has the revolution already started?
  • To save lives, we must turn to nuclear energy
  • The U.S. must step up its efforts with Ukraine
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 7 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-07 03:29AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus: too early to suggest arrival of sixth wave, Hong Kong leader Carrie Lam says amid rising number of cases
  • Hong Kong counterterrorism unit set to launch hotline for tip-offs on suspicious activities with cash rewards for useful information
  • Hong Kong police to mobilise all officers for possible state leader visit at handover bash, force chief says
  • Hong Kong police chief warns viewers to avoid documentary about city’s social unrest over concerns of possible national security violations
  • Funding for Hong Kong child protection group cut by HK$1.8 million after abuse allegations
  • Hong Kong’s top officials could be put under week-long ‘closed-loop’ arrangement ahead of 25th handover anniversary celebrations
  • Former Hong Kong Bar Association chairman tipped to become justice minister in John Lee’s governing team, with incumbent Teresa Cheng on the way out
  • Hong Kong’s economy to further improve if city’s Covid-19 situation remains under control, says finance chief
  • Beijing’s foreign ministry in Hong Kong hits back at Western consulates over messages marking Tiananmen Square crackdown anniversary
  • Alleged driver charged after online video shows car mounting safety island, travelling against flow of traffic in Hong Kong
  • Man slashed in knife attack in New Territories village, Hong Kong police search for suspects
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong health experts warn of rebound in cases, as city logs 543 new infections
  • China plans sweeping makeover of academic journals to raise the profile and influence of domestic scientific research
  • Kuomintang chairman, in Washington, pitches his party as stabilising presence between Beijing and Taipei
  • China’s foreign minister in Kazakhstan as Ukraine, Afghanistan uncertainties rumble
  • China’s unemployment woes see Yunnan offer big cash subsidies to entice college graduates
  • Chinese scientists say ‘Midas’ touch helps to fine-tune CRISPR gene editing
  • EU lawmakers push to designate human rights conditions in Xinjiang as ‘genocide’
  • US suspends tariffs on some solar panel imports for two years, but leaves China out
  • How the economic slowdown and zero-Covid threaten China’s global ambitions
  • China accuses Canada of stepping up reconnaissance against Chinese military
  • Coronavirus: Chinese tourists trickle back during Dragon Boat Festival, but far short of pre-pandemic levels
  • Shanghai reopening: Tesla’s Chinese buyers face up to 24 weeks for delivery as carmaker scrambles to catch up with demand in ‘China’s Motown’
  • Covid infections lower in people with food allergies – and not higher in asthmatics, study finds
  • Covid outbreak shuts down Chinese city as Beijing and Shanghai return to new normal
  • Taiwanese military set to get first set of locally made short-range drones as PLA threats intensify
  • With China’s big political reshuffle looming, new No 2 propaganda chief is appointed from party academy
  • June date set for China-sponsored Horn of Africa peace conference
  • Australia’s lettuce shortage forces KFC to put cabbage in its burgers
  • Singapore airfares cost 27 per cent more than before Covid-19 – so why are prices so high?
  • China ‘testing’ Tokyo with underwater survey off Japan’s coast, analysts say
  • Sri Lanka’s Rajapaksa to finish term despite protests: ‘I can’t go as failed president’
  • Indian products pulled by Kuwait supermarket in row over Prophet remarks
  • Australia’s Albanese, Indonesia’s Jokowi ride bamboo bikes, talk ‘green’ investment, trade and language support
  • At least 26 dead in India’s northern state after bus falls into gorge
  • Indonesia woos digital nomads with 5-year remote working visa, and promotes spiritual getaways, to bring in longer-staying visitors
  • Lawrence Wong becomes Singapore’s No 2 leader, cementing position as Lee Hsien Loong’s successor
  • In Australia, one man is on the hunt for the world’s most dangerous spider
  • Coronavirus: Singapore should prepare for a new Covid-19 wave in July or August, health minister says
  • Malaysia palm group warns of losses ahead from ‘severe’ labour crunch
  • ‘Nothing less than hate speech’: Google ordered to pay Australian politician US$515,000 over defamatory YouTube videos
  • Shock as Iraq jails British man Jim Fitton for 15 years over smuggling artefacts
  • Gupta brothers accused of defrauding South Africa arrested in UAE
  • Two-year-old US boy shoots and kills father after finding gun at home
  • UK’s Boris Johnson survives no-confidence vote after partygate rebellion
  • Taser-maker Axon halts controversial project to equip drones with stun guns
  • Top Gun heirs sue Paramount over Maverick sequel
  • Ukraine war: US orders two Roman Abramovich jets seized over Russia sanctions
  • Mass shootings now define American exceptionalism
  • Ukraine recovers steel plant siege bodies from last-ditch stand in Mariupol
  • South Africans visiting UK on Ryanair quizzed in Afrikaans to prove identity
  • ‘New Kazakhstan’ backs sweeping reforms to end strongman Nazarbayev’s era
  • Some people still haven’t caught Covid-19. Why?
  • US must course-correct on gun violence after Texas school massacre to save itself
  • UK to give Ukraine long-range M270 rocket systems after Putin warnings
  • Russian general reportedly killed in eastern Ukraine, another high-ranking casualty
  • Russian foreign minister Lavrov’s trip to Serbia cancelled as neighbours close airspace
  • Ukraine’s President Zelensky visits close to war front line near Sievierodonetsk
  • Tencent-backed Chinese start-up G7 Connect and rival E6 Technology merge to create truck management services giant
  • Hong Kong real estate owners seek alternative uses for retail complexes and hotels amid tourism slump
  • Climate change: UK-backed initiative aims to tackle greenwashing with globally recognised emissions standards
  • What Singapore can teach Canada about curbing home prices without the xenophobia
  • Chinese carmakers get V-sign from analysts as tax cuts seen reviving share prices, sales from April slump
  • Elon Musk says Twitter’s lack of information on bots breaches terms of their merger deal
  • China’s box office receipts hit 10-year low as ongoing Covid-19 restrictions produce a sad storyline for country’s cinemas
  • Didi to resume app downloads and new user registrations as Beijing security probe nears completion: report
  • Banks, technology giants see growing number of skilled employees leaving for flexible fintech firms, new analysis shows
  • Fiscal spending a better tool than monetary easing to boost China’s economic growth
  • Amazon cloud’s latest collaboration with Hong Kong science park seen as vote of confidence in city’s role as regional tech hub
  • When Stephen Hawking visited Hong Kong in 2006, famed scientist had a rock-star welcome at the airport
  • Hong Kong has always had expats who found the city incredibly boring
  • 10 summer health tips from Ayurveda experts to avoid excess body heat and stay healthy
  • 2022 LVMH Prize jury rewarded young designers, and Cate Blanchett, Louis Vuitton’s Nicolas Ghesquière and Delphine Arnault were there with advice and support
  • Why depression in old age is different, and how to deal with it – in yourself or a loved one
  • Irma Vep, HBO drama series starring Alicia Vikander, sees director Olivier Assayas revisit theme of his 1996 film with fascinating results
  • Myanmar artist Aung Myint’s evolving style on show in Hong Kong exhibition
  • 2 unique places to visit in Malaysia but which of Penang’s Blue and Green Mansions is best? We compare tours, price and overall experience
  • Inside Sourav Ganguly’s new US$5 million family home: the ‘Prince of Kolkata’ and Indian cricket legend splashed out on his first move in 48 years from his hometown of Behala
  • How royal titles will change when Prince Charles becomes king: Queen Elizabeth just celebrated her Platinum Jubilee, but what will William, Harry and Kate Middleton be called in future?
  • Who is the father of Amber Heard’s baby? Meet daughter Oonagh Paige: during the Johnny Depp case, the actress spoke out about being a mum, with plenty of fans wondering if Elon Musk is the baby daddy
  • How Madonna’s son David Banda is following in her footsteps: the 16 year old from Malawi made headlines by wearing an Adidas x Gucci dress as tribute to his famous mum – and he sings and dances too
  • 5 most exciting films premiering in June, from the third Jurassic World movie Dominion, to Pixar’s Lightyear and Netflix sci-fi feature Spiderhead starring Chris Hemsworth
  • The 13 best Japanese restaurants in Hong Kong: Sushi Wadatsumi, Shikigiku Japanese Restaurant, Sushi Saito at Four Seasons Hong Kong, Ryota Kappou Modern and Zuicho are all leading the wave
  • How the high jewellery market boomed in Asia during Covid-19: Chanel, Cartier, Bulgari, Van Cleef & Arpels and Graff used Zoom and private viewings for clients to maintain relationships
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: NY Times: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Battered by Russian Shells,a Monastery RemainsLoyal to Moscow
  • Ukraine Insurgents Strike in Russia-Occupied Regions

  • British pound and stocks rise as vote on prime minister washes over markets.

  • A Post-Roe America

  • Britain’s prime minister, beset by a scandal over lockdown parties, faces a decisive vote.

  • Russia Seeks Buyers for Plundered Ukraine Grain, U.S. Warns
  • Big City Mayors, Furious About Mass Shootings, Fear Sweeping Gun Limits Are Out of Reach

  • A Broken Redistricting Process Winds Down, With No Repairs in Sight
  • Mother Charged With Manslaughter After Her 2-Year-Old Fatally Shoots Father
  • Louisiana House Map Is Blocked by Judge Who Calls It a Racial Gerrymander
  • Jessica Cisneros Calls for Recount in Texas Runoff Against Rep. Cuellar

  • Arizona Officers on Leave After Watching Man Struggle and Drown

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: Guardian: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Thousands of Italian mafia operating in Australia, federal police say
  • Bulusan volcano eruption dumps ash on Philippines – in pictures
  • Comedian John Oliver offers to buy Melbourne’s ‘demonic’ banana statue
  • ‘Out in a year’: what the papers say about Tory vote on Boris Johnson
  • Russia-Ukraine war: what we know on day 104 of the invasion
  • Doc Antle of Tiger King charged with laundering $505,000
  • Israeli coalition dealt a blow with loss of West Bank settler law vote
  • ‘Every second counts’: wife of British journalist missing in Amazon urges action
  • Archivists rush to preserve records of atrocities under Ferdinand Marcos Sr
  • How to win pub trivia: ‘A good team is a bit like a political cabinet’
  • Proud Boys leaders charged with seditious conspiracy in 6 January riot
  • Sally Rooney novels reset expectations of Ireland, says TV director
  • Nearly half of Republicans think US has to live with mass shootings, poll finds
  • Rider Davy Morgan dies in third fatal crash at this year’s Isle of Man TT races
  • Southgate says Euros final racism adds ‘layer of difficulty’ to shootout plans
  • Climate delegates snub Russian address as activists fear geopolitical setbacks
  • Prince Harry and Meghan release photo of Lilibet on her first birthday
  • Gupta brothers arrested in Dubai over alleged corruption in South Africa

  • Trudeau calls China’s close encounter with Canadian warplanes ‘provocative’
  • Federal judge authorizes US to seize two of Roman Abramovich’s private jets
  • The PM is safe for now – but the vote is hardly a ringing endorsement
  • Day of pleading and threats ends with Boris Johnson in post. Just about | John Crace

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
